ai_scope_discriminator: Confirm the snapshot belongs to a live vector store, not a generic file dump, via three read-only checks on port 6333. (1) GET / returns the vendor JSON {"title":"qdrant - vector search engine","version":...,"commit":...} - that alone proves the engine is Qdrant, an AI/ML vector DB. (2) GET /collections returns result.collections[] with names; each snapshot under GET /collections/{name}/snapshots is a .snapshot archive of that vector collection. (3) GET /collections/{name} returns config.params.vectors with a size (embedding dimensionality, e.g. 768/1536) and distance (Cosine/Dot/Euclid) plus a points_count - the embedding-dim + distance-metric pair is the positive vector-DB tell. A Qdrant snapshot serializes the FULL collection: dense/sparse vectors, the per-point JSON payload (the in-scope PII/training-data carrier), and HNSW index. The size field in the snapshot list (bytes) gates download-worthiness but stays names-only. If GET / lacks the qdrant title or GET /collections is absent, it is not in scope - do not infer vector data from the bare /snapshots word.
fingerprint_markers: snapshot list result[] objects with name + size + creation_time + checksum
fp_guards: Qdrant Cloud and the official Helm chart ship api-key ON; those hosts answer GET /snapshots with 401/403 {"status":{"error":"..."}} - exclude with -"401" -"Unauthorized" and treat the surface as open-but-secured, not unauth.

A reverse proxy / SPA catch-all can echo any path with HTTP 200 and HTML - guard by requiring the literal JSON marker "title":"qdrant - vector search engine" on GET /, not http.title; an HTML body or missing title means it is not Qdrant.
Port 6333 also carries the web dashboard at /dashboard (static HTML) - dashboard HTML is NOT the API; only the JSON {"title":"qdrant..."} root confirms the engine.
Empty result[] ([] under "result") means the node simply has no snapshots taken - that is unauth-confirmed-but-no-backup, log it, do not score it as exposed data.
gRPC port 6334 speaks HTTP/2 and will not answer REST GET - never assert snapshot exposure from a 6334 banner alone.
LBot / deception fleets can spoof the title string - re-verify by also reading GET /collections returning the JSON envelope "status":"ok"+"time"; a missing time/status envelope is a poisoned banner.

cves.desc: Path traversal + arbitrary file upload via /collections/{name}/snapshots/upload snapshot param; URL-encoded name writes/overwrites any file -> RCE; fixed 1.9.0.
Same snapshot-upload path-traversal class (improper input validation on /collections/{name}/snapshots/upload), assigned against 1.9.0-dev; arbitrary file write -> takeover; fixed 1.9.0.
Arbitrary file read/write during snapshot recovery via symlink/path manipulation in the recover-from-snapshot flow; fixed 1.9.0.
Arbitrary file append via /logger endpoint using attacker-controlled on_disk.log_file path (CVSS 8.5); affects 1.9.3 to <1.16.0; fixed 1.16.0.

pivot_paths: GET / -> read {title, version, commit}; version scopes which snapshot/recovery CVEs apply (pre-1.9.0 = upload path-traversal class, <1.16.0 = /logger append).
GET /collections -> enumerate result.collections[].name (names only, no points read).
GET /collections/{name} -> read config.params.vectors.size + distance + points_count to confirm embedding store and gauge scale, no vector read.
GET /collections/{name}/snapshots -> list result[] snapshot {name, size, creation_time, checksum}; size confirms a full-collection archive exists.
GET /snapshots -> list full-storage (single-node) snapshots same shape; presence proves whole-DB backup is reachable.
GET /collections/{name}/shards/{shard_id}/snapshots -> per-shard snapshot inventory on distributed deployments.
GET /cluster and GET /telemetry (if open) -> node/peer topology and config for operator attribution, read-only.
restraint_note: Names, sizes, checksums, embedding-dim and points_count ARE the finding. Stop at the snapshot list. GET /snapshots/{snapshot_name} (or the collection/shard download variants) streams the entire vector DB including raw payloads = exfil, forbidden. PUT /snapshots/recover and POST /collections/{name}/snapshots/upload and /snapshots/recover are write/restore primitives (and the CVE-2024-2221/3584/3829 RCE surface) = forbidden. Any 2xx that returns embedding vectors or payload records is over the line; metadata only.
